summary: Read and analyzed local file <machine-local-path> (1).md as requested. Verified SHA-256 matched expected value 4B8C7447D4048781935EC7D8B8223112953941BEB4060AA0661AD6D298AE4DE7. No user/source file edits were made. Analysis focused on review queue automation, semantic job settlement, behavior-trigger rail, Slack/notification receipt, and deterministic-vs-semantic boundaries.
decisions: Treat this as a read-only/file-only analysis, not a counted 20-agent validation vote requiring a fresh full SecondBrain read.

Confirmed the plan consistently separates deterministic automation from semantic judgment and blocks final readiness on unclassified residual work, missing evidence, unsafe notification routing, stale status, or JSON-only proof.
Use trace_only because this was a read-only audit/review task.
next_steps: If implementation begins, start with the plan's ordered gates and keep P1-07/P1-08/P1-09 tied to route/evidence/owner, Korean UI proof, and final-readiness blockers.

Do not treat review queue count reduction, completed semantic job counts, or Slack ledger entries as success unless residual classification, evidence, owner/next action, and visible Korean proof are present.
